creator: yuri@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Roger Mason from comment #0)

Hello Roger,

Thank you for your report.

Are you saying that simply importing these fails:
> import spglib
> import pymatgen
> from abipy import abilab
?

How about only one pymatgen?
> import pymatgen

Importing these three doesn't fail for me:
[yuri@yv /tmp]$ python2.7 test-abipy.py 
/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pymatgen/__init__.py:89: UserWarning: 
Pymatgen will drop Py2k support from v2019.1.1. Pls consult the documentation
at https://www.pymatgen.org for more details.
  at https://www.pymatgen.org for more details.""")
/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pymatgen/analysis/elasticity/tensors.py:7: UserWarning: pymatgen.analysis.elasticity.tensors has been moved to pymatgen.core.tensors, please update dependencies accordingly. Links will removed in v2019.1.1.
  warnings.warn("pymatgen.analysis.elasticity.tensors has been moved to "
[yuri@yv /tmp]$
